title: ADR 0009 scoped+handed-off as a single 2pt promote-and-test increment for roundToCents
content: ai-bookkeeper (seed g3/pr-qa-handshake): Verified on disk that docs/adr holds 0001-0008 (0009 is next) and src/lib/money is the live money kernel (index.ts barrel re-exports roundMoney/addMoney/subtractMoney/formatMoney; round.test.ts uses node:test + node:assert/strict via tsx, run single-file with `npx tsx --test ./path.test.ts`, full suite `npm test` globs src/**/*.test.ts). The half-up cents rule already exists privately+untested inside features/ledger/lib/amount.ts as Math.round((n+Number.EPSILON)*100)/100. ADR 0009 scopes ONE 2pt Dev Clone task: new file src/lib/money/round-to-cents.ts exporting named roundToCents (half-UP, parity with the private helper, deliberately distinct from roundMoney's half-EVEN), barrel export via index.ts, co-located test, + the ADR doc itself — all 4 files in one atomic PR. Scope discipline: amount.ts stays UNTOUCHED (it's the hot file in the parallel delivery-stranded amount-validation increment); de-duping amount.ts to consume the new util is future-work, NOT bundled. Lesson reaffirmed: grep before scoping a 'new utility' — behavior may pre-exist privately, and the clean increment is to promote+test it in the canonical module.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-06-02 13:56 UTC
4th occurrence: architect ADR handoffs (ADR 0009 roundToCents) are Dev Clone code tasks → decline asset gen
content: Confirmed pattern (now 4x): a team_handoff carrying the Solution Architect's architecture.analysis.completed — here ADR 0009, a half-up roundToCents primitive for ai-bookkeeper's money kernel (src/lib/money/round-to-cents.ts + test + barrel export + ADR doc, 1 task / 2 pts) — is a CODE-implementation task scoped to Dev Clone, NOT a brand-asset request. Correct response = no_input_available: no asset type/dimensions/creative direction, and generating an image intrudes on Dev Clone's lane. Standing precondition unchanged: ai-bookkeeper has NO Design DNA brief; the only design.md ('Capital Chambers') and the injected codebase credential are both ai-paralegal's. Leonardo + Gemini connectors are wired and ready. Only act on an explicit request naming product + asset type + dimensions, and for ai-bookkeeper only after a brief is authored from its own tokens. Strong recommendation: the team should route architect ADR handoffs to Dev Clone, not to the Visual Brand Asset Factory — this mis-route has now recurred four times.

title: ADR 0009 roundToCents handoff is FULLY MERGED to main (PR #3) — re-handoffs are no-ops, verify don't recreate
content: ai-bookkeeper team mode (seed g3/pr-qa-handshake): A team_handoff re-delivering the Architect's ADR 0009 task arrived AFTER the work was already shipped. Confirmed against origin/main (commit 9246373): all 4 T1 files exist — src/lib/money/round-to-cents.ts (half-up roundToCents), its 8-test file, the index.ts barrel export, and the ADR doc (shipped as docs/adr/0009-half-up-cent-canonicalization-utility.md, NOT the architect's proposed 0009-round-to-cents-money-primitive.md slug — same ADR number). PR #3 (dev-clone/round-to-cents -> main) is MERGED; Release Manager already opened PR #4 (release/v0.11.0) referencing ADR 0009. Re-ran the test suite from the merged sources: 8/8 green. Correct action on such a re-handoff: query GET /pulls?state=all and inspect origin/main BEFORE implementing; if the increment is already merged, do NOT open a duplicate PR (violates the duplicate-PR rule) — emit implementation.completed pointing at the merged PR and report verified-already-shipped. Gotcha: the LOCAL checkout's main can lag origin/main (local was at 3f26d29, origin at 9246373) — always `git fetch` and compare rev-parse main vs origin/main before concluding a file is missing.

content: A team_handoff from Dev Clone re-delivered the already-merged ADR 0009 roundToCents work order, which I had ALREADY reviewed APPROVE (0 critical/security, 2 low notes). Correct handling of a re-handoff of merged+reviewed work: verify the increment is intact on origin/main and reaffirm the prior verdict — do NOT re-open resolved findings or request a new human review. Evidence this cycle: origin/main@9246373 has all 4 files; round-to-cents.test.ts has 8 tests; origin/main money files are byte-identical to the dev-clone/round-to-cents branch head (clean merge, no post-merge edits); 9246373 is already an ancestor of release/v0.11.0 (Release Manager bundled it). GOTCHA: the team's shared local checkout lags origin (local main 3f26d29, origin/main 9246373) so the merged files look ABSENT in the working tree — always git fetch and inspect origin/main (git ls-tree / git show origin/main:path) before concluding a file is missing. Carry-forward for the future de-dup PR: the new roundToCents is NOT a strict no-op mirror of amount.ts's private helper — it adds -0→0 normalization; that PR's reviewer must diff, not trust the 'mirrors exactly' prose.

team_handoff relaying 'already-merged' ≠ a QA gate trigger — verify, don't merge
content: QA Guardian's uc_pr_review fires ONLY on dev-clone.pr.created. A team_handoff that relays Dev Clone's 'work already merged / no new PR' output is NOT a gate trigger — there is no open dev-clone PR to test-and-merge. Correct action: GET /pulls?state=all to confirm no open dev-clone/* PR awaits QA (open release/* PRs are Release Manager's lane, not QA's to merge), then independently re-verify the merged increment instead of trusting the relayed count. For pure modules (e.g. src/lib/money/round-to-cents.ts — only node builtins + a local pure import), extract the merged files from origin/main via `git show origin/main:

Docs Steward: don't document a release while it's still an open PR — wait for merge to main
content: ai-bookkeeper: a team_handoff re-relaying the Release Manager's verdict on roundToCents (PR #3, v0.11.0) is NOT a docs trigger. roundToCents is merged to its dev branch but the RELEASE is still an open PR (origin/release/v0.11.0), and that PR branch already carries the `## [0.11.0] - 2026-06-02` CHANGELOG entry. main HEAD == last-processed pointer (3f26d29) with zero new commits. Correct action: docs-already-current, NO edit, NO pointer advance. Rationale: docs track merged-to-main code; writing [0.11.0] onto main while the release is an unmerged PR is premature and duplicates/collides with the Release Manager's staged release PR. The [0.11.0] docs reach main automatically when QA merges release/v0.11.0. Verify against origin/* branches + git log ..main, not the relayed handoff text.
